name: Caramel-Vanilla Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 2 cans sweetened condensed milk (14 ounces each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the sweetened condensed milk into two 8-inch square baking dishes.
2. Cover each dish with aluminum foil.
3. Place the dishes in a larger shallow pan filled with hot water to a depth of 1/4 inch.
4. Bake at 425 degrees for 1 hour and 20 minutes, or until the sweetened condensed milk is thick and caramel colored. Add hot water to the pan as needed during baking.
5. Remove the foil and let the caramelized milk cool.
6. In a saucepan, combine sugar, half-and-half, and whipping cream. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly.
7. Gradually stir about one-fourth of the hot mixture into the eggs, then add it to the remaining hot mixture, stirring constantly.
8. Cook over medium heat for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
9. Remove from heat and stir in the caramelized milk. Let cool thoroughly.
10. Pour the cooled caramelized milk mixture into a freezer can of a 1-1/2 gallon hand-turned or electric ice cream freezer.
11. Add milk and stir well.
12. Freeze according to the manufacturer's instructions. Let ripen for at least 1 hour.
